place: Cranbury CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Cranbury's historic Cranberry Mills showcases the town’s 18th‑19th century mill town heritage.
The Cranbury Historical and Preservation Society saved the 1713 East Jersey Cottage, relocating it in 2019 to its original 1693 tract.
In 1869 the town and its brook were renamed Cranbury, marking a formal name change from earlier designations.
The Second Presbyterian Church of Cranbury, founded in 1838, stands as one of the town’s oldest religious institutions.
Alexander Hamilton and the Marquis de Lafayette used a Cranbury home as a Revolutionary War headquarters, visited by George Washington in 1778.
The Cranbury Historical and Preservation Society’s 1734 deed of sale is preserved in the History Center, offering a rare glimpse into early property records.
